text: ‘The channels, with their high banks’; or perhaps (according to the Hebrew text) ‘the channels of Egypt’.

vv. 7-10. Several phrases in this passage are of doubtful interpretation, particularly the last verse; the Hebrew text here being understood by some as meaning ‘The pillars of the land (that is, the nobles) shall be crushed, and those who work for hire will grieve’.
The Latin implies that Egypt is the corner-stone of her own commonwealth, but the Hebrew text can equally well be interpreted as applying that description to the great men of the country.
The Hebrew text here may mean, not that the Egyptians will serve the Assyrians, but they will serve (i.e. worship the true God)with the Assyrians.
